data.name: textBox1.Text
data.value:
- Making a Community Post is not possible at this time. Youtube dev api does not give an endpoint to use for it. However, if google does decide to open up the api for publishing them via API, i will update this app to support them. For post scheduling on other platforms, use other apps. I also plan to build an automation app for that aswell.
- Scheduling a schedulable video is also not possible, that is what this program is partly for. This might be added in the future.
- Adding end screens is also no possible, due to same reason as community posts - no dev-facing api end points, the program instead, has option to remind you to configure the end screens after the video has been uploaded as an OS message.
- Sorry for programmer UI
- This program is Licensed to you under the GNU GPLv3, see more in enclosed file

data.name: richTextBox2.Text
data.value:
Prefabs work kinda like upload defaults. They are essentially some presaved entry data that has been configured ahead of time, by the app operator (YOU) in order to save time on creating entries. Except unlike on youtube, you get to have multiple different ones, if you have multiple different types of content to do.
How to use this mini-editor-in-editor:
- Go to "Prefab editor", fill out the info that you want to have selectable. Unlike when creating an entry, everything here can be empty.
- Save your prefab under a specific name.
- Select a prefab on the "Add entry" tab from the prefab combobox
Note: Prefabs do not allow you to set the video file location nor the thumbnail location. Aswell as using a prefab after filling those fields WILL clear those fields again.
- To delete or see prefab info, go to manage prefabs.
- To modify an existing one, select a prefab at the top of the prefab editor.

data.name: richTextBox1.Text
data.value:
Hi, I am a YouTuber, who needed tools to assist in a mostly solo journey.
I have the ability to program, yes, though I did use AI on this specific app. I used it to direct the development. I rewrote the code manually, requested understanding on Forms details and adapted the code more to my suiting.
Ive learned better to use Forms elements thanks to this, aswell as how to apply a MVC-like structure to a desktop app which is not a browser wrapper. The result is this "MFC" or "Model-Forms-Controller" structure.
Why Desktop app?`I wanted this tool to be independent of any online service, pipelinetool or external connection other than what this is intended for.
What is this intended for? It is intended to aid content creators in delivering their content to youtube - free of charge, free of content shadowbans and with the source code and source prompts.
Yes - you can read exactly what I asked from AI, compare the code in the source vs AI, and make your own modifications.
This was fun for me to make, AI makes for a great "duck on the deck".
I hope you hold this in your hands with good intentions backed by better means. - Yours, Estlib
https://chatgpt.com/share/6a5012d4-a978-83eb-a239-dca21e0430f0
